data.title: ASML removed from top picks at Cantor Fitzgerald after weak guidance
data.description:
• ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) was in the spotlight again on Thursday after Cantor Fitzgerald removed the chip equipment maker from its top picks, following its weak guidance earlier this week.
  • Shares rose 0.5% in premarket trading but have dropped 17% over the past five trading sessions.
  • ASML's shares are likely in the "penalty box for a bit" until investors can regain confidence for growth into 2026, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C. J. Muse said. As such, he believes shares will be range-bound between €600-700.
  • Muse kept his Overweight rating, however, as shares are "near trough-like levels" and there is a path over the next 6 to 12 months that could see the stock return to roughly €750.

data.title: General Motors in talks to move Asian manufacturing to Mexico
data.description:
In an effort to reduce imports into the country from Asia and increase domestic manufacturing, Mexico’s deputy head of industry and trade, Vidal Llerenas, is in talks with several industrial giants including General Motors (NYSE:GM), Foxconn, and Intel (INTC) to begin manufacturing locally.

GM (GM) and Foxconn are expected to announce plans to swap out products manufactured in Asia for production in Mexico later this month, Llerenas recently said to Reuters, adding that the Mexican government is also currently in talks with Stellantis (STLA) and DHL. Intel (INTC) already plans to substitute 12% of the products it ships to Mexico to begin local manufacturing.

Of the total imported vehicles into Mexico, 40% come from Asia, according to industry data with General Motors (GM) the number one importer at 65% made in China, followed by Sweden’s Volvo (OTCPK:VLVLY) at 54%, and Ford (F) with 24% of all imported vehicles into Mexico made in China.

As recently as 2022, calling it "the most convenient thing to do," GM (GM) moved manufacturing of the Chevy Onix from Mexico to China, exporting it back to Mexico rather than import the Brazilian made Onix to Mexico. Production of other models destined for Central American markets also moved from Brazil to China, rather than being manufactured in Mexico.

data.title: Total Energy Services files to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Total Energy Services (TSX:TOT:CA) said it has filed a notice with the Toronto Stock Exchange to conduct a normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the normal course issuer bid, the firm may purchase up to 1.91M shares.

data.title: ECB cuts key interest rates by 25 bps
data.description:
The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year, after policymakers flagged reduced inflation risks.

Markets now closely watching President Christine Lagarde's speech to gain more insights on the central bank's strategy.

 

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (HEWG), (DAX), (FLFR), (FLGR), (FLIY)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Allstate estimates September catastrophe losses at $889M, vs. $272M in August
data.description:
• Property and casualty insurer The Allstate (NYSE:ALL) estimated September catastrophe losses to come in at $889M, or $702M after-tax.
  • ALL's August estimated catastrophe losses stood at $272M, or $215M after-tax.
  • The September figure includes $630M, pre-tax, related to Hurricane Helene.
  • For Q3, catastrophe losses were $1.70B, or $1.35B after-tax.
  • For the first nine months of 2024, catastrophe losses were $4.55B or $3.60B, after-tax.
  • ALL shares were trading +0.18% Thursday pre-market at $196.01.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ShiftPixy acquires TurboScale in $150M deal
data.description:
• ShiftPixy (NASDAQ:PIXY) said it has acquired AI tech company, TurboScale in a $150 million deal.
  • The deal consisted of $75 million in stock and $75 million in debt.
  • The acquisition will help ShiftPixy to introduce AI-powered features.
  • The company also expects accelerated growth and innovation after the acquisition, and is in plans to roll out new AI-driven tools by early 2025.

data.title: Lucid stock slides after pricing securities offering
data.description:
Shares of Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) fell Thursday morning after the company priced its previously announced securities offering.

The offering will be conducted from time to time in one or more transactions. The automaker plans to sell 262.44M shares of its common stock and has granted the underwriter, BofA Securities, a 30-day option to purchase up to 39,367,040 additional shares of its common stock.

The company also said its majority stockholder, Ayar Third Investment Company, an affiliate of Saudi Arabia's Investment Fund, intends to purchase more than 374M shares in a private placement at the same price per share initially paid by the underwriter in the public offering. As a result of these purchases, Ayar expects to maintain its ~58.8% ownership of Lucid's outstanding common stock.

Lucid (LCID) expects to raise around $1.67B from the stock offering and the corresponding private placement. Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, among other things, capital expenditures and working capital.

Shares of Lucid Group (LCID) fell 17% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: American Rebel regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• American Rebel (NASDAQ:AREB) said it has received a formal notice from the Nasdaq, stating that the company has regained compliance with the minimum bid price rule.
  • Press Release

data.title: SentinelOne investor day provides more confidence, Needham says
data.description:
• SentinelOne (NYSE:S) held its first-ever investor day, and research firm Needham left the event impressed on the company's execution and financial targets.
  • Shares rose 0.9% in premarket trading.
  • "We come away more confident in SentinelOne’s expanding platform, enhanced go-to-market, and improving execution," analyst Matt Dezort wrote in a note to clients.
  • Dezort, who has a Buy rating and $32 price target on SentinelOne, noted the day was "full of product announcements," including enhancements to Purple AI, updates to data and security information and event management, and the releases of Singularity Hyperautomation and Ultraviolet large language models. It also reiterated financial targets that it made when it went public and said cloud annual recurring revenue has topped $100M and data and security information and event management has topped $70M, above industry trends.
  • "Given Lenovo's leadership position in PC shipments, the new partnership could be transformational, and along with [SentinelOne's] improved positioning following the outage, could drive compelling upside to new ARR estimates over the coming years," Dezort added.

data.title: Wipro GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line, revenue of $2.66B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Wipro press release (NYSE:WIT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line.
  • Revenue of $2.66B (-1% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Total bookings was at $3,561 million. Large deal bookings4 was at $1,489 million, an increaseof 28.8% QoQ and 16.8% YoY in constant currency
  • Voluntary attrition was at 14.5% on a trailing 12-month basis.
  • Wipro’s Board of Directors recommended issue of bonus shares to shareholders (includingstock dividend to ADS holders) in the ratio of 1:1 (1 equity share for every 1 equity share held),subject to approval of shareholders.
